content.problemStatement:
Alexey is buying a new apartment! To pay for it, he is going to take one or more loans.

There are nnn banks in the city. The iii-th bank offers loans up to sis_isi‚Äã rupees with a daily compound interest rate of pip_ipi‚Äã. He gets all the loans on Day 0.

Thereafter, on each day, Alexey can afford to spend at most ttt rupees on repaying the loans (and the interest!). He wants to finish repaying all the loans completely within the next mmm days (or faster). What is the maximum amount that he can take out on loan on Day 0?

So, the full process is as follows:


Alexey takes any non-negative real amount of rupees 0‚â§xi‚â§si0 \le x_i \le s_i0‚â§xi‚Äã‚â§si‚Äã from each bank. Sum x1+‚ãØ+xnx_1 + \dots + x_nx1‚Äã+‚ãØ+xn‚Äã is what we want to maximize.
On each of the next mmm days, as long as there are loans remaining, the following happens: The remaining amount in the iii-th loan, if it is yet to be completely paid off, gets multiplied by (1+pi1000000)(1 + \frac{p_i}{1000000})(1+1000000pi‚Äã‚Äã). Then Alexey can distribute up to ttt rupees between the loans to repay them. He can partially repay them as well.

Input Format

The first line will contain three integers: nnn, mmm, and ttt.
The second line consists of nnn integers: s1,s2,‚Ä¶,sns_1, s_2, \ldots, s_ns1‚Äã,s2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,sn‚Äã.
The third line consists of nnn integers: p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pnp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pn‚Äã.


Output Format
One floating point number -- the answer to the problem. If your output is within an absolute or relative difference of 10‚àí610^{-6}10‚àí6 from the optimal answer, it will be judged to be correct.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Suppose we loan Rs. 5 from the first bank on Day 0.
Then on Day 1, the loan becomes 5√ó(1+0.22)=6.15 \times (1 + 0.22) = 6.15√ó(1+0.22)=6.1. We then repay Rs. 2, and the amount becomes 4.1.
On Day 2, the amount increases to 4.1√ó1.22=5.0024.1 \times 1.22 = 5.0024.1√ó1.22=5.002. We then repay Rs. 2 and it becomes Rs. 3.002.
On Day 3, the amount increases to 3.662443.662443.66244, and after we pay, it decreases to 1.662441.662441.66244.
On Day 4, it increases to 2.02817682.02817682.0281768, and on repayment becomes 0.02817680.02817680.0281768.
On Day 5, it increases to 0.0343756960.0343756960.034375696, and we repay it completely on this day.

Hence, by the end of the 5th day, we have completely repaid all the loans. Hence we can loan Rs. 5 and meet the requirements. But this is not the maximum possible. The maximum possible is 5.7272795225825.7272795225825.727279522582, which we loan from the first bank.

At the end of Day 1, it becomes 4.987281017550044.987281017550044.98728101755004.
End of Day 2, 4.08448284141104884.08448284141104884.0844828414110488.
End of Day 3, 2.9830690665214795362.9830690665214795362.983069066521479536.
End of Day 4, 1.639344261156205033921.639344261156205033921.63934426115620503392.
On Day 5, after the increase because of interest, the amount becomes 1.99999999861.99999999861.9999999986, and we repay it in full after that.
